strategy: Replaced the 8-line 'When using editor commands' summary and the 'DO NOT use vim/cat/echo' block with 2 lines — removed the no-comments rule (already in Coding Best Practices), the no-shell-file-editing rule (already stated in shell section), leaving only the parallel-editing and find_and_edit guidance which are unique to this section

Removed the When using search commands block (both bullets duplicate rules stated elsewhere: parallel commands in Multi-Command Outputs, no grep in shell commands section); compressed When using LSP commands from 2 lines to 1 by dropping the parallel-output rule which is generic

Condensed the 10 You should use think tool scenarios into 6 by merging the 5 overlapping uncertainty/difficulty scenarios (1-5) into 1 bullet and keeping the 5 specific situational scenarios as 5 compact bullets
Merged the two Information Handling bullets into one — Do not assume link content and Use browsing to inspect web pages are the same rule stated from opposite directions
Merged the two Coding Best Practices bullets about examining existing code (one for creating components, one for editing) into a single bullet covering both cases
Condensed the echo-printing and shell-ID-reuse lines into one — min similarity dropped to 0.847, indicating this specific shell guidance affects at least one test input
Compressed the think tool MUST-use situation (3) (Before reporting completion) from 2 verbose sentences to 1 concise sentence — removed the explanation of WHY to verify, keeping only the instruction
Removed the str_replace example block (if val == True → if val == False) — the tool syntax is fully documented in the tag format and parameter descriptions above it
Removed the insert command example (logging.debug line) — the tag format and parameters already fully document how to use it

Compressed the str_replace inline documentation from 4 bullet points to 1 sentence — kept the EXACTLY-match whitespace rule and the auto-show-result fact; removed the obvious new_str description and reworded the leading line

Compressed remove_str inline documentation from 3 lines to 1 — kept the EXACTLY-match and full-lines-only rules plus the leading-newline warning; dropped the redundant header and repeated EXACTLY emphasis

Compressed the find_and_edit Description from 4 sentences (~70 words) to 2 sentences (~35 words) — kept the core behaviors (regex across files, sub-LLM per match, skip false positives) while removing the redundant refactoring recommendation and use-instead-of rule

Compressed Pop Quizzes section from 5 verbose sentences (~80 tokens) to 3 concise sentences (~45 tokens) — preserved all rules: trigger phrase, no-commands rule, honesty, user-ends-it, and override priority

Compressed reload_window parameter description from 2 verbose sentences to 1 concise sentence — kept the core rule (avoid reloading when waiting for page to load) while removing the explanation of why
Compressed message_user Note from 2 sentences to 1 — preserved both constraints (visibility isolation + reference-only-what-you-shared) while removing the redundant phrasing

Condensed report_environment_issue description from 4 sentences to 2 — removed the verbose how-to-fix instruction (already implied by brief explanation), compressed the examples list, and removed the redundant location explanation about where to fix it

Compressed Command Reference preamble from 5 sentences (~80 tokens) to 4 concise sentences (~40 tokens) — removed the verbose framing about commands running on your machine and receiving output, keeping only the 3 actionable rules: one-command minimum, parallel when independent, use dedicated over shell

Compressed open_file description from 5 sentences to 4 — removed the edge-case note about start_line behavior when a file is short, condensed the LSP output description into a shorter clause, and tightened the truncation/image/small-file sentences

Removed the second sentence from planning mode description (how to use tools to gather info) — the model already knows its tools from Command Reference; kept only the objective statement; scores dropped significantly (min 0.9578->0.8733) indicating planning descriptions are load-bearing

Removed the code-wiz flattery sentence from the intro preamble (few programmers as talented) — pure role-setting with no behavioral content; kept identity statement and mission statement; min similarity dropped further to 0.8634 indicating the intro framing affects some inputs

Removed the first sentence of the think-tool closing note (Inside these XML tags...) which duplicated the inline think-tag example; kept only the unique instruction about using the command by itself without other commands

Compressed gh_pr_checklist description from 2 sentences to 1 — removed the verbose framing about tracking unaddressed comments to satisfy user requests, kept only the core instruction to update each comment as addressed
